input:
**The problem**
When I start cloning/downloading a repository with GitHub Desktop and then delete/cancel the download before it finishes, the download continues in the background.

Even though the repository no longer appears to be downloading in the UI, network activity continues and the clone eventually completes in the background.

**Expected behavior**
Cancelling or removing the repository while it is still downloading should immediately stop the clone process and terminate any associated ne
expected_summary: Customer reports that cancelling a repository clone does not actually stop the download.
notes: Real GitHub issue, cleaned of template scaffolding.

input: After updating to 3.6.0 in noticed that scrolling in the Repository list (which includes over 40 non-GitHub Repositories) occasionally doesn't allow to scroll up or down and is kinda "sticky" to the current position. It seems to  move a few inches up and down, but gets stuck in the original location after i stopped scrolling.
expected_summary: Customer reports the repository list occasionally gets stuck and won't scroll after updating the app.
notes: Real GitHub issue, cleaned of template scaffolding.

input:
When a user is not logged in, clicking the pull request refresh button produces no visible response. The button is not disabled and no error message is shown — it simply does nothing.

The refresh button should either:
- Be disabled/hidden when no user is logged in, or
- Display an error/prompt indicating that login is required

Note: Whatever behavior should communicate to the user.

The button appears interactive (not disabled) but clicking it has no effect and provides no feedback to the user
expected_summary: Customer reports the pull request refresh button does nothing when they are not logged in, with no error shown.
notes: Silent failure (no error message) -- tests whether 'nothing happens' still reads as technical, not general.

input:
We've received multiple reports recently about GitHub Desktop and other applications built on Electron crashing at startup. We're still investigating this but [users have reported that they've been able to resolve this](https://github.com/desktop/desktop/issues/22184#issuecomment-4592111909) by removing a faulty SID (security identifier) from their %LOCALAPPDATA% folder that was seemingly left there by Google Antigravity.

We'll be keeping this issue pinned as we gather more information about th
expected_summary: Customer reports the app crashes on startup for users who also have a specific third-party tool installed.
notes: Root cause involves a third-party interaction; tests generalization beyond simple single-app bugs.

input:
I found out that you can copy the query executed by a button if you select it with your mouse while using ctrl+c. This is not a major issue but still isn't intended behavior.
Signal 8.8.0 on Ubuntu 26. Reproducible at will.

1. G to any chat
2. Click with your mouse
3. Don't release the click and hover above the microphone button near the message bar
4. Press ctrl+c to copy
5. Paste the content

Your clipboard shouldn't have changed

6.
expected_summary: Customer found a minor UI bug where selecting a button while pressing ctrl+c copies internal button data instead of expected content.
notes: Real GitHub issue, cleaned of template scaffolding.

input:
I've had multiple instances recently where pinned messages do not appear as pinned on Signal Desktop. In these cases, I believe I had not opened Signal Desktop in a day or so since pinning the messages.

1. Pin a message in a chat on phone
2. Wait a day
3. Open signal desktop, observe that the messages sync but not the pin status

The pinned message state should sync across platforms

In some cases, Signal Desktop does not sync the pinned message status from the phone

_No response_

8.7.0

macO
expected_summary: Customer reports pinned messages don't always show as pinned after not opening the app for a day or so.
notes: Real GitHub issue, cleaned of template scaffolding.

input: After ending calls in Signal Desktop, the microphone access indicator appears with the ringing and stays on indefinitely. This started happening about 2 weeks ago. In waybar, the indicator shows that Signal (`Ringrtc`) is still accessing the microphone even after the call has ended. In previous versions, the microphone access would be released immediately when the call ended.
expected_summary: Customer reports the microphone indicator stays on indefinitely after ending calls, a recent regression.
notes: Real GitHub issue, cleaned of template scaffolding.

input:
CTRL-SHIFT-L has been the keyboard shortcut to open the chat menu for several years.

Since 7.81, this keyboard shortcut no longer works.

Initially reported in https://community.signalusers.org/t/beta-feedback-for-the-upcoming-desktop-7-82-release/73328/5?u=hiqua and ack-ed by the team, but opening here in case a contributor feels like fixing this.

1. Go to any chat
2.
expected_summary: Customer reports a keyboard shortcut that used to open the chat menu no longer works after an update.
notes: Real GitHub issue, cleaned of template scaffolding.
